segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an upbeat, quirky background tune accompanying the title card, 'Abbott Elementary: Principal Ava’s Most Unhinged Moments Part 1.' The scene transitions to the exterior of Willard R. Abbott Elementary, where Principal Ava, an animated woman in a white coat, enthusiastically greets staff members with "Good morning!" Her staff, including Gregory, respond before Ava proudly announces that "The district was so moved by my plea that they approved the emergency budget and sent us the money right away," eliciting surprised "Oh!"s and "Hey!"s from the group. She explains they could have hired aides or gotten rugs, but she wanted "something more immediate." Janine, a staff member, interjects that "the rugs are immediate," like "instant Xanax for kids," having explained it in an email, which Jacob questions. Ava then shifts topics, stating she always feels better with her hair done, leading to better work, and that fixing the "outside" makes the "inside take care of itself," accompanied by a whoosh sound effect. The camera pans to a close-up of the school’s green sign, featuring the name 'Willard R. Abbott Elementary' and a stylized woman. Inside a library or meeting room, with staff seated around a table, Ava asks, "Y'all seeing this? A plastic sign." She reveals, "Thank God for the school district 'cause they gave us $3,000 and I had to spend all of it" on the sign. Melissa exclaims in disbelief, "You spent all of the money on this?" Ava proudly defends it as a "Rush job. Can you believe its quality?" Gregory sarcastically asks, "How's that optimism taste?" before Mr. Johnson questions, "Why are we here exactly?" to which Ava dismissively replies, "Well, chocolate drop." The quirky background music continues throughout the lively and comedic exchanges.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a well-lit library or meeting room, adorned with 'Dream BIG' and 'ACHIEVE' posters, where a diverse group of staff members, mostly women in professional attire and ID badges, are seated and standing. Principal Ava, a woman with long blonde hair in a white coat and patterned top, animatedly leads the discussion, her clear, assertive female voice declaring, "I learned that someone here doesn't respect me... how can you respect this school?" Her lively expressions and gestures engage the attentive staff, whose reactions range from amusement to surprise. A male staff member's questioning voice asks, "Who doesn't respect you, Ava?" to which she responds, "It's not important. We're going to make this a group matter." She then proposes an exercise, inviting them to "say whatever we want out loud," and addresses a female staff member, "Let's start with Janine." Janine, in a soft, hesitant voice, replies, "Yes," only for Principal Ava to describe her as "pushy, squeaky, and annoying," prompting Janine's surprised "Excuse me?" and another male staff member's interjection. Ava quickly clarifies the "constructive" nature of the exercise before calling on "Hershey Kiss," a male staff member who reluctantly states, "I really don't want to." As Ava tries to involve "Jacob, Barbara," and another staff member interjects, she firmly takes control, stating, "No one's doing this to anyone. Sheena, come on in." A female staff member expresses concern, "Ava, that is my student. She should be at lunch," as a young boy with curly hair and glasses, wearing a light blue school polo, approaches. He speaks in a soft, slightly whiny child's voice, confirming, "I am kind of hungry," and Principal Ava places a hand on his shoulder, gently asking, "Sheena, remember what we talked about?" reinforcing the warm, community-focused atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a bustling library or meeting room where staff members are gathered, some seated with food, while others stand. A woman with curly hair, wearing a blue floral blouse and a lanyard, identified as Ava, speaks animatedly, stating something 'be a bad thing' before asking 'where is everybody going?' A male voice responds that they are 'to check on Janine,' followed by another male voice adding 'to eat lunch, after I check on Janine.' A calm male voice advises everyone to 'stay calm,' but Ava excitedly interrupts, declaring, 'This is it, y'all! The end times!' and exclaiming 'it's happening!' The scene abruptly shifts to a dimly lit basement or storage area. Here, a woman with a headlamp searches cluttered shelves, while others, including a woman in a green sleeveless top, look concerned. A female voice shouts, 'Don't shake the ladder!' as Ava, now on a ladder and inspecting the ceiling with a flashlight, is watched intently by the group. A male voice states, 'Gregory is the only person that can stay in my bunker, so stop asking.' The woman in the green sleeveless top, with a distinct voice, then asserts, 'Hell yeah, I'm a doomsday prepper,' questioning why more people aren't and referencing 'Train to Busan' and 'fast zombies,' adding, 'That day is coming. That day soon comes.' Ava, from the ladder, calmly clarifies, 'Ava, it is just a partial power outage,' then instructs everyone to 'listen up!' A male voice echoes, 'Listen up, y'all!' and a female voice asks, 'Are you kidding me?' Ava continues, explaining the plan: 'everybody without power, please, head to the gym. We will conduct classes there until this is all fixed. It is not ideal, but it will work.' A male voice urges, 'You heard her, let's go!' Finally, a woman with short black hair, wearing a patterned blazer, apologetically states, 'I'm sorry everybody, I just thought if I could get up here and get this done, we wouldn't have to wait,' to which another female voice responds, 'And look where it landed us, baby girl.'

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a dimly lit basement where staff, including the Woman in Green Sleeveless Top and the Woman in Striped Shirt with Headlamp, are investigating. A male voice directs everyone to the gym, stating, "we've got bigger fish to fry now." Amidst the urgency, a frantic male voice exclaims, "Oh Jesus, my Branzino! Everybody out of the way!" as the Woman in Beige Turtleneck, later identified as Janine, appears startled and gestures, admitting, "I just... I feel lightheaded." A concerned female voice asks if she's eaten, and another male voice adds she missed breakfast. The scene cuts to the Man in Pink Shirt crouched beside the Person on Floor, illuminated by a flashlight, as a female voice urgently declares, "Okay, we're losing her. Do I have your consent to slap you?" followed by "There she goes! She's out!" and a collective gasp, confirming the person has passed out. The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse humorously reacts, "Oh my god! She pale like a zombie! You know they eat the hottest people first. Let me back my tasty ass up." The setting then transitions to a brightly lit library where staff are eating and conversing. Janine (Woman in Beige Turtleneck) asks, "Why are you all looking at me like that?" The Woman with Short Black Hair then addresses Janine directly, revealing, "Janine, sweetheart, you have only dated one man your entire life." Janine, standing with a tense expression, asks, "No women? Nobody else ever?" A male voice comments, "That just answers so many things." The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse animatedly demands, "Somebody give me a chair!" amidst laughter. Janine then asks, "Is... is the guy rich?" to which the Woman with Short Black Hair replies, "No, not monetarily. He's an aspiring musician." The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse interjects, "So he broke. Somebody give me a fresh chair!" The Woman with Short Black Hair defends him, saying, "Look, you don't know Tariq. He's sweet, he's caring, he's ambitious." On-screen text identifies the episode as 'Work Family,' Season 1, Episode 8.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a library/meeting room where staff members are gathered. The Woman in Blue Floral Blouse, identified as Barbara, speaks animatedly, stating that 'Jacob knows' and later teasing the Man in Dark Blazer, Jacob, about needing 'a little bit more help than that.' Jacob, wearing a lanyard with 'Willard R. Abbott Elementary' visible, describes himself as 'very spirited,' 'confident,' and a 'real extemporaneous thinker.' Barbara continues to playfully question their friendship, asking if he's 'secretly Mormon' after he beatboxes and says 'one joystick,' to which Jacob humorously replies, 'No. Oh, you should be 'cause you need more man.' Barbara then calls out her own name, 'Barbara.' The Woman with Short Black Hair, listening intently, addresses the Woman in Beige Turtleneck, Janine, saying, 'I'm sorry, Janine, but that was actually very clever.' Janine, with a startled expression, speaks urgently, suggesting, 'Get all your desks to the gym until this whole thing blows over' and exclaiming that 'life is a joke, people.' The scene shifts to a hallway with brick walls. Jacob urges, 'Come on, hurry up. Who knows when those kids are start robbing?' The Woman in Black Lace Top, leading a conversation, states, 'They get here at 7:30.' The Woman in Green Sleeveless Top reacts with surprise, asking, 'Every day? That's wild.' The Woman in Black Lace Top then firmly demands to see 'these permission slips to the zoo,' warning, 'they better be real 'cause I can tell if you fake the Herbie Hancock.' The Woman in Green Sleeveless Top corrects her, 'Oh, it's John Hancock. Girl, I know.' The segment concludes with Janine, now smiling, guiding a group of children in school uniforms through the hallway, holding papers.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a school hallway with beige brick walls, where a group of children in school uniforms are being guided by the Woman in Beige Turtleneck, who wears a lanyard and gestures as she speaks. A male voice, identified as the Man in Mustard Shirt, declares, "I just say whatever I want," followed by the Woman in Beige Turtleneck expressing concern about "mishaps" and not having filled out paperwork for the school district. Another female voice, possibly the Woman in Green Sleeveless Top, asks, "Wait, then why are we going?" to which the Man in Mustard Shirt replies he wants to go to the zoo. The scene shifts to a classroom where Principal Ava, in a yellow and brown striped dress with a lanyard, interacts warmly with the Young Boy in School Uniform, who holds a stuffed animal and smiles as she hands him a plate with a sandwich. A male voice, identified as the Man in Grey Blazer, announces he's "returning Micah" after time in his office. Another male voice, identified as the Man in Plaid Shirt, asks Micah if he has anything to say to the class, to which the Young Boy, Micah, replies, "Not really." The Man in Plaid Shirt then asks what he wants him to say, and the Man in Grey Blazer demands an apology for disrupting the class and a promise it won't happen again. The Man in Plaid Shirt sarcastically retorts that it *will* happen again and asks if he wants him to lie, to which the Man in Grey Blazer says "No." The Man in Plaid Shirt reminds Micah, "Don't forget your actual lotto," and then asks him a riddle: "what does Axolotl do on vacation?" Micah responds with a pun, "Relaxolotl," which the Man in Plaid Shirt affirms. The setting then transitions to a bright, informal staff lounge or break room, identified by on-screen text as 'Principal's Office' from Season 2, Episode 4, where a group of staff members, including the Man in Grey Blazer and the Man in Plaid Shirt, are seated at tables eating and conversing. The Man in Grey Blazer, wearing a grey cardigan over a mustard shirt, speaks animatedly while gesturing, complaining that "Ava Lidley" made him take candy from a baby and didn't discipline Micah at all. The Man in Plaid Shirt, in a red and brown plaid shirt, listens and responds, countering that "Ava doesn't discipline the kids." The Man in Grey Blazer then asks why he was told to send them to her. The Man in Plaid Shirt reminds him of his previous advice, explaining that "sending a kid to Ava just helps get him out of the room so that you can regain control of the rest of the class." The Man in Grey Blazer argues that "it doesn't fix the problem and rewarding bad behavior..." Other staff members, including a Woman in Green Sleeveless Top and a Woman with Red Hair, are also present, some holding phones or food, contributing to the casual, collaborative atmosphere, with kitchen appliances and vending machines visible in the background.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a staff lounge where a group of staff members are seated around a table, eating and conversing casually. Amidst the informal atmosphere, a male voice is heard asking "What y'all doing in here?" before dismissively adding "Never mind, I don't care." Visually, Principal Ava, in a yellow and brown striped dress, is seen handing a sandwich to a young boy in a school uniform, while other staff members react with amusement or surprise. The scene then transitions to an office setting, accompanied by the distinct sounds of a door opening, closing, and footsteps. Principal Ava, now wearing a solid yellow blouse and her 'Willard R. Abbott Elementary' lanyard, is seated at her desk, which is adorned with personal items like a framed photo and a Philadelphia Eagles flag. She is engaged in a serious, intense conversation with a man in a dark blue polo shirt, later identified as Mr. Eddie. Their dialogue is already underway, with Mr. Eddie's voice accusing Ava of being "the opposite of what a principal should do," to which Ava challenges him to "repeat what you just said." Mr. Eddie then asks, "You wanted to see me?" and Ava confirms, "Why yes, Mr. Eddie. I did, though ideally not under these circumstances." Their discussion intensifies, with Ava stating she's made it a point "not to harass you these past few weeks," and Mr. Eddie countering that Ava does "these kids a disservice" by not being a "strong authority figure." He emphatically declares that "Kids are supposed to be scared to go to the principal's office. You turned it into a Chucky Cheese!" A door slams as Ava vehemently retorts, "Don't say that! I got beef with every Chuck E. Cheese in the greater Philadelphia area." Mr. Eddie, displaying expressive gestures, asks to leave, "Can I go? Have you not embarrassed me enough?" Ava, with equally expressive facial expressions, continues the heated exchange, describing the emotional distress of children making a "walk of shame" to her office, with "snot running down their face" and "shaking like a chihuahua," questioning if she's "supposed to punish them" or "tune out some sobbing child" while trying to watch something, as her speech is cut off. The camera alternates between close-ups of their faces, emphasizing the emotional weight of the conversation under bright, professional lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens outside a bustling red brick school building, filled with children in school uniforms, some holding blue and white 'TRUMBULL PENS & PENCILS' boxes, suggesting a fundraiser. Amidst the lively chatter and general schoolyard sounds, a woman in a light grey coat with a fur collar, identified as "Principal Coleman" by a female voice, addresses the "students" with an assertive tone, stating she'll "take the lead today" with "new tactics" but warns them "do not forget everything that I taught you." Another male voice interjects, advising to "forget everything she taught you. Toss it right out of the window," before giving a "first lesson" to "act like you've already made a sale," and calling out "Courtney, you're with me." Principal Coleman then engages a man in a brown jacket and grey flat cap, asking him, "How many bars did you say you wanted?" and clarifying, "how many candy bars did you tell this adorable child that you would be purchasing?" The man, looking down, responds, "Fine. I'll take one. You got change?" Principal Coleman sarcastically replies, "Some people might ask for change, but those people aren't Sam Cook, so change is not gonna come." A young girl in a brown coat with pigtails, holding a box of pencils and smiling, chimes in with a cheerful "Sorry, we don't have change," followed by a distinct "yoink" sound. As the camera follows the girl past a yellow 'SLOW WATCH FOR CHILDREN' sign on a brick pillar, Principal Coleman sharply blows a red whistle, causing the children to pause and a woman in a black leather coat, now identified as "Barb," to react with surprise, holding up her phone. Principal Coleman continues her "next lesson," advising, "Nobody likes to buy things from happy people, so I suggest you come up with an illness for yourself. My go-to is polio." The man in the brown jacket interjects with a "A, no," to which Principal Coleman responds, "Barb, polio is back. Poke yourself in the eyes if you have to. Tears might as well have dollar signs in them." She adds, "And you're gonna wanna look out for liberal white people." The man in the brown jacket then states, "Actually, I consider myself to be a progressive," prompting the young girl to ask, "Is there a difference?" The atmosphere remains lively but slightly chaotic, with adults trying to manage the crowd.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens on a bustling outdoor school scene, with children in school uniforms gathered near a red brick building. The Man in Brown Jacket and Flat Cap, seen walking through the crowd, is heard at the outset discussing sales tactics, stating, "You'll be able to spot them because they'll be making this face," and adding, "If you can't sell candy bars to them, you're unteachable." Amidst this, a child's voice exclaims "Ow!" prompting the Woman in Grey Coat with Fur Collar, identified as Barbara, to ask, "Malika, are you okay?" Malika, a child, responds, "No, but I'd feel a lot better if you bought some candy from me," and then attempts a manipulative sales pitch, "An at-risk black youth." Barbara, visibly animated and gesturing, blows a red whistle to manage the children, then sternly addresses the Woman in Black Leather Coat, identified as Ava, saying, "All right, that's it. You've gone too far. May I have a word with you, Ava?" Ava, holding a phone, responds with surprise, "I thought you wanted me here for an educational emergency?" Barbara then reprimands Ava, accusing her of turning students into "scammers" with "aggressive" and "predatory" tactics, likening it to a "check-cashing store." Ava, whose expression shifts from surprise to concern, pragmatically defends her methods, stating, "This is how you break through the noise." Barbara, however, sets firm conditions for a compromise: "No lying, and no racial profiling!" The lively background includes children, like the Young Girl in Brown Coat, holding boxes labeled 'TRUMBULL PENS & PENCILS', underscoring the fundraising context of the intense discussion between Ava and Barbara.

A woman in a green turtleneck reacts with curiosity, asking "You mean carrot cake?" before expressing disgust, "just looking at them almost put me over the top." Jacob then addresses a woman in a beige blazer as Janine, offering her the "carrot cookies" made with "salvaged oat pulp," to which Janine politely declines, stating she "probably shouldn't on an empty stomach." As Jacob walks away, a blaring fire alarm siren abruptly fills the hallway, prompting the woman in the grey coat to question if it was on the schedule before taking charge, shouting "Single file line, most flammable kids first!" and "Everybody out! This is a real fire!" The camera follows the woman in the green turtleneck as her expression shifts to concern while she walks down the hallway, her hands on her hips. The hallway is filled with children in school uniforms, some carrying backpacks, moving amidst the chaos. The woman in the beige blazer then leads a group of children, including a young girl in a purple jacket, down the hallway, while the woman in the green turtleneck follows behind, observing the scene. The well-lit environment, decorated with colorful artwork and heart-themed decorations, underscores the active school setting, now in a state of emergency. On-screen text identifies the scene as 'Fire' from Season 2, Episode 15.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic, handheld shot moving through a bustling school hallway, capturing students and staff in motion amidst a cacophony of urgent shouts and dialogue, including someone asking, "Ava, where are you going?" and a voice declaring, "I'm protecting my piece, the firefighters are almost here." The camera then cuts to the exterior of Willard R. Abbott Public School, where children in school uniforms are gathered near the entrance, some holding boxes labeled 'TRUMBULL PENS & PENCILS,' as a silver Lexus drives past. Amidst the sounds of a car horn and continued commotion, a voice directs, "All right y'all, come on, safely right there," and "Get the door," emphasizing the urgency. The scene transitions to a library or meeting room where staff members are seated at tables, engaged in a discussion. Principal Ava, now in a mustard yellow blouse and glasses, listens intently, her expression shifting from serious to surprised. The conversation shifts to a less chaotic tone, with a woman asking, "She's got coming back issue?" and another responding, "No." The camera pans across the room, showing other staff members, including the Woman in Black Leather Coat and the Woman with Short Black Hair, reacting. A voice, likely the Woman in Black Leather Coat, addresses Principal Ava, asking, "Not planning on being conscious for career day?" The staff then discusses a "district initiative," with various voices expressing opinions, one person finding them "more annoying than usual" and another stating, "I feel like they've been unequivocally and universally worse than they've ever been," describing them as "unhelpful to unbearable." Suddenly, cheerful children's voices are heard greeting, "Good morning, Abbott Elementary!" followed by a sigh. The Woman in Black Leather Coat then firmly tells Ava, "I've already told you I'm not interested in auditioning for the Golden Bachelorette. Gerald is still very much alive." The scene shifts to a classroom where a woman in a maroon dress with a patterned scarf stands near a chalkboard, gesturing. The final shot shows a woman in a black 'HARVARD' sweatshirt holding a clipboard, smiling confidently in a classroom, and is heard saying, "Actually, I'm here about your desk. When I was at Harvard..." The overall atmosphere moves from chaotic movement to focused discussion and then to a calm, educational setting, punctuated by distinct voices and background sounds.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a brightly lit classroom, featuring colorful rugs, desks, and a large 'PENMANSHIP' chart. A woman in a maroon cardigan, white blouse, and patterned scarf (the Woman in Maroon Dress with Patterned Scarf) stands animatedly, gesturing while speaking in an instructional tone about "communal seating" being "better for kindergartners' development." She addresses another woman, the Woman in Black 'HARVARD' Sweatshirt, who holds a clipboard, wears glasses and a silver necklace, and listens with a mix of defiance and amusement, responding in a dry, slightly sarcastic tone that she prefers her "desk separated" for ease of movement. The Woman in Maroon Dress then adopts an exaggerated, high-pitched, mocking voice, mimicking a child named "Little Jonathan" who "never learned how to make direct eye contact" and "can't hold down a job," to which the Woman in Black 'HARVARD' Sweatshirt dryly agrees to "take that into account." The Woman in Maroon Dress firmly declares it a "District mandate" that "cursive is no longer in the curriculum," followed by a sound of papers being thrown or a desk being hit. The scene then cuts to an office with large windows, a desk cluttered with binders and a potted plant, and a modern, professional feel. A woman in a striped shirt with red and black vertical lines (the Woman in Striped Shirt, later identified as Ava) enters, holding papers, muttering "Damn it" in frustration. She confronts another woman, Janine (the Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse), who is seated at the desk wearing a black blazer over a white V-neck top. Janine calmly greets Ava, who then exclaims, "No! What the hell?" Janine, with a disapproving and firm tone, expresses disbelief that Ava has "a picture of Gregory in your desk drawer," prompting Ava to retort sarcastically. Janine then directly asks what Ava is doing there, to which Ava assertively states she's "here with a demand." Janine corrects her, suggesting she means "a request," but Ava insists she needs "a company card." After a brief back-and-forth clarifying if she means "card" or "car," and Janine's blunt refusal, Ava explains her urgency in trying to get "an ASL translator for Jacob's class." Janine, now agreeable, offers to hire one and put it "on the company card," but Ava clarifies that Janine is "not getting a company card," leaving Janine exasperated and trailing off, questioning the point of Ava being there. On-screen text identifies the segment as 'Gregory's Garden Goofballs,' Season 3, Episode 3.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in an office setting where the Woman in Striped Shirt, wearing a white shirt with red and black vertical stripes, is seated at a desk, holding papers and speaking with urgency, her voice heard denying she's a 'mole' and asserting her importance. A male voice interjects, calling her 'more like a pimple.' The Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse, dressed in a black blazer over a white V-neck top, stands across from her, reacting with a stern expression and stating she's trying to help a student. The male voice then announces he's going to get 'free fancy bubbly waters,' which the Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse challenges, asking if the clear bottle labeled 'SANDY DO NOT DRINK' she holds was taken from the communal fridge, to which the male voice claims he brought it from home. The scene shifts to a school hallway where the Woman in Black V-Neck Blouse, now wearing a navy blue dress with a lanyard, walks briskly while holding a phone and a beige coat, her concerned expression matching the sounds of footsteps and distant chatter. She passes a Young Boy in School Uniform sitting on a wooden bench with a blue backpack, who looks up at her. As she rushes down the hallway, joined by a group of staff members including the Woman in Green Turtleneck, the Man in Plaid Shirt, and the Woman with Red Hair, all displaying expressions of alarm, a woman's excited voice exclaims, 'I just saw a ghost y'all!' followed by a cacophony of excited, overlapping voices discussing the 'ghosts' multiplying. They converge at a reception desk where the Man in Grey Blazer, whose calm voice is heard introducing himself and his son Charlie, states they are looking for Principal Coleman and that it's Charlie's first day, having enrolled via the district website. The Woman in Beige Blazer stands with him, holding the Young Boy. Amidst the panic, a woman's voice urgently asks if others see 'them too,' and another jokingly suggests getting a 'proton pack' before being told to 'stop.' The scene cuts to a different hallway where the Woman in Brown Turtleneck, wearing a brown top with green and white trim and a lanyard, speaks with urgency, her voice explaining that a big-time PGA golf course is being built about ten blocks away, which is 'allegedly good for the city' and popular. The final shot transitions to an outdoor construction site, showing a yellow CAT skid-steer loader moving dirt near a large circular sand pit, with workers and a 'WATER RENOVATION' sign visible in the background, indicating ongoing school grounds improvements.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens in a bustling staff lounge where a man in a striped sweater, holding a mug, urgently asks a gesturing woman with red hair if she touched something, to which she defensively explains she sent someone home for ringworm and sanitized surfaces. Their conversation is quickly amplified by other staff, including a woman in a striped shirt and another in a maroon dress holding an orange, who emphatically declare "That's not enough!" A woman (likely the Woman in Striped Shirt) then warns that "Jacob, ringworm is in elementary school's worst enemy," emphasizing its rapid spread among children. A male voice counts "One, two, three," followed by a relieved "Thank God," as the scene transitions outdoors. A 'MAX Express' delivery van is parked by a brick wall adorned with a 'MOTIVATION' mural. A woman in a blue top unloads boxes, handing one to a surprised woman in a purple cardigan with pearls, while a man in a brown jacket approaches. Amidst a vehicle screech, the woman in the blue top shouts to "Bring 'em inside!" and criticizes the delivery with "Zero stars! Did he just leave everything on the sidewalk like this?" She then instructs, "Okay, good, you guys are here. We don't have insurance, so lift with your legs, brace your back." The woman in the purple cardigan, identified as Ava, retorts, "Ava, my back does not brace. Call that young man back. You got his phone number, Barb?" to which the woman in the blue top (Barb) replies, "I will dial 411 before I pick up." A male voice humorously interjects about "Jesus" being lean and muscular. The scene then shifts indoors to a classroom or office where a woman in a green dress walks in with a serious expression. A man in a dark jacket and glasses, holding a metal cup, stands looking concerned as he addresses someone off-screen, asking "Who you talking to?" and then, in a deep, calm voice, "You. What do you want?" He continues, stating, "I know there wasn't a lost ticket and I know that the district..." before the audio cuts off, leaving a tense atmosphere. Throughout, lanyards are consistently visible, reinforcing the school setting.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene unfolds in Principal Ava’s office, where she, dressed in a mustard yellow blouse and ID badge, is seated at her desk, engaged in a tense conversation with the Man in Dark Jacket, who stands across from her in a dark jacket and glasses. He begins by firmly stating, "not give you these computers." Principal Ava, gesturing animatedly, responds with frustration, "Damn it. Who gave it away? Barb?" The Man in Dark Jacket reveals, "It was Principal Coleman." Ava, her expression shifting to surprise, retorts defensively, "Well, that can't be right. That's me." He leans forward, explaining in detail that the computers' advanced processor confirmed his suspicions, questioning accusatorily, "Where did you get these?" Ava scoffs, defiantly asking, "You think I got in this chair by folding under pressure?" The Man in Dark Jacket, maintaining a firm tone, warns her that if she doesn't tell him, he'll have to assume they're stolen and report it. After a brief pause, Ava, still gesturing, explains, "We got the rich people building the golf course to donate them." The Man in Dark Jacket questions with slight incredulity, "You got them to?" Ava, exasperated and with rising emotional intensity, finally admits, "Fine. It was blackmail, black man, a bribe, quid pro quo!" The camera alternates between close-ups of their faces and medium shots, emphasizing their strong emotional engagement and the gravity of their exchange within the soft, professionally lit office setting.
